knownIssues.title: 144Hz mode requires powerful GPU
knownIssues.description: Running at 144Hz significantly increases GPU load. Most systems should use 120Hz for the best balance of smoothness and visual quality.
knownIssues.workaround: Use 120Hz unless you have an RTX 3080+ class GPU. 90Hz is fine for demanding games.

knownIssues.title: Lighthouse base stations can cause USB bandwidth issues
knownIssues.description: Base station firmware updates and some configurations can compete for USB bandwidth, causing tracking dropouts.
knownIssues.workaround: Ensure base stations are on latest firmware via SteamVR. Use bluetooth for base station power management rather than USB.

knownIssues.title: Tether cable is fragile and expensive to replace
knownIssues.description: The Index tether cable is a single proprietary cable carrying DP, USB, and power. Repeated twisting damages it.
knownIssues.workaround: Use a cable management system (pulley) to reduce cable stress. Avoid stepping on or sharply bending the cable.

optimizationTips.tip: Use 120Hz for the best performance-to-smoothness ratio
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: 120Hz gives you incredibly smooth VR without pushing your GPU as hard as 144Hz. Most people can't tell the difference.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: 120Hz requires 8.33ms frame time vs 6.94ms for 144Hz — a 20% reduction in GPU budget. SteamVR motion smoothing at 60fps→120fps is more effective than 72fps→144fps. Use 120Hz with motion smoothing enabled for the best perceived fluidity.

optimizationTips.tip: Set supersampling to 100% and let SteamVR auto-adjust
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Let SteamVR handle the picture quality automatically. It knows what your GPU can handle.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: SteamVR's automatic resolution scaling targets the Index's native resolution at 100% SS. Manual supersampling above 130% provides diminishing returns due to the Index's lower pixel density. GPU resources are better spent on higher refresh rate.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable direct display mode in SteamVR
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Direct display mode makes your headset bypass Windows desktop rendering, reducing delay.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: SteamVR Direct Display Mode bypasses the Windows DWM compositor, eliminating 1-2 frames of latency. Verify enabled in SteamVR → Developer → Direct Display Mode. The Index should not appear as a monitor in Windows Display Settings.
